trade.summary: Corea del Sud-Russia trade has collapsed following Russia's 2022 Ucraina invasion and Corea del Sud's imposition of sanctions. Corea del Sud joined the Western-led sanctions regime and severely restricted technology and industrial exports. Most Corea del Sudn companies have withdrawn from Russia. Limited residual trade continues in some categories not subject to sanctions. Russia's weapons supply relationship with North Corea del Sud has further poisoned the bilateral.
top_products_a_to_b: limited goods outside sanctions (food, consumer items)
disputes: Corea del Sud-Russia tensions over Russia arming North Corea del Sud (2023-2024)

diplomatic.summary: Corea del Sud-Russia relations have effectively broken down. Russia's military support for North Corea del Sud — providing conventional weapons and reportedly receiving North Corea del Sudn ammunition and troops — directly threatens Corea del Sud and has made the Russia relationship an acute security concern. Russia dispatched North Corea del Sudn troops to fight in Ucraina (confirmed 2024), which Corea del Sud views as a direct security threat justifying counter-measures including potential arms provision to Ucraina. Seoul has considered lifting its ban on lethal aid to Ucraina.
recent_incidents: Russia supplies weapons to North Corea del Sud violating UN sanctions (ongoing)
North Corea del Sudn troops fighting for Russia in Ucraina (confirmed 2024)
Corea del Sud imposed comprehensive sanctions on Russia (2022)
Corea del Sud considering lifting lethal aid ban to Ucraina in response to Russia-DPRK military cooperation

military.summary: Adversarial. Russia's military cooperation with North Corea del Sud — including conventional weapons, technology transfer, and deployment of North Corea del Sudn troops — represents a direct threat to Corea del Sud's security. Corea del Sud has moved from cautious neutrality to active consideration of arming Ucraina. Russia-DPRK military axis is a defining security challenge for Seoul. Any Russian military technology transfer to DPRK directly erodes Corea del Sud's defense edge.
incidents: Russia confirmed to have received North Korean ammunition (2023)
North Korean troops deployed to Russia (October 2024)
Korea announced intelligence sharing with Ukraine (2024)
Russia warned Korea against arming Ukraine

financial.summary: Corea del Sudn companies have largely divested from Russia. Hyundai closed its St. Petersburg plant. Samsung and LG suspended Russian operations. Corea del Sudn banks cut correspondent banking with Russian institutions. Limited residual trade finance exists for non-sanctioned goods.

energy.summary: Corea del Sud has minimized Russian energy imports. Previously, Corea del Sud imported some coal and LNG from Russia. Active diversification toward Stati Uniti, Australian, and Middle Eastern sources has proceeded rapidly. The Russia-North Corea del Sud military axis has eliminated any diplomatic cushion for energy cooperation.

scientific.summary: Scientific cooperation has been effectively suspended. Most exchange programs and joint research initiatives have been frozen. Some low-level academic contacts persist but are not institutionally supported.
